definitions.text: An elongated piece of wood or similar material, typically put to some use, for example as a wand or baton.
examples: The beaver's dam was made out of sticks.
I found several good sticks in the brush heap.

definitions.text: A small, thin branch from a tree or bush; a twig; a branch.
examples: The beaver's dam was made out of sticks.

definitions.text: A relatively long, thin piece of wood, of any size.
examples: I found several good sticks in the brush heap.
What do you call a boomerang that won't come back? A stick.

definitions.text: To cut a piece of wood to be the stick member of a cope-and-stick joint.
To compose; to set, or arrange, in a composing stick.
To furnish or set with sticks.
The tendency to stick (remain stuck), stickiness. The traction of tires on the road surface.
The traction of tires on the road surface.
That which sticks (remains attached to another surface). The amount of fishing line resting on the water surface before a cast; line stick.
To become or remain attached; to adhere.
examples: The tape will not stick if it melts.
The lever sticks if you push it too far up.
A non-stick pan. A stick plaster.
A sticker type of glue. The stickest kind of gum.

definitions.text: The customary length (according to the material used) of a piece or roll of textile fabrics imported from Flanders.
